title: Tryonia myriophylla
intro: Tryonia myriophylla, also known as Lippia myriophylla, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tryonia myriophylla include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the production of essential oils and extracts for pharmaceutical and cosmetic applications.
